# servicenow_ui_policy_action (Resource)

`servicenow_ui_policy_action` manages a UI policy action within ServiceNow. UI policy actions are child records of a UI policy that specify which fields are affected and how (visible, mandatory, disabled).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages an action attached to a UI policy in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_ui_policy" "example" {
  short_description = "Make assignment_group mandatory when priority is critical"
  table             = "incident"
  conditions        = "priority=1^EQ"
}

resource "servicenow_ui_policy_action" "example" {
  ui_policy  = servicenow_ui_policy.example.id
  field_name = "assignment_group"
  visible    = "true"
  mandatory  = "true"
  disabled   = "ignore"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `field_name` (String) Name of the field this action targets.
- `ui_policy` (String) Sys ID of the parent UI policy this action belongs to. Cannot be changed once created.

### Optional

- `cleared` (Boolean) If true, the field's value is cleared when the policy condition is met.
- `disabled` (String) Whether to set the field disabled (read-only). Valid values are 'true', 'false', or 'ignore'.
- `mandatory` (String) Whether to set the field mandatory. Valid values are 'true', 'false', or 'ignore'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `visible` (String) Whether to set the field visible. Valid values are 'true', 'false', or 'ignore'.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_ui_policy_action.example <sys_id>
```
